skill.name: executing-plans
skill.description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
skill.summary: Execute written implementation plans in reviewed batches with verification checkpoints.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: The static high finding for weak cryptography at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive. The file contains workflow instructions only, with no cryptographic algorithms, executable code, network access, or data exfiltration behavior. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Match for Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3. That line is only the skill description and contains no cryptographic algorithm, code execution, or secret handling. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is direct evidence that the flagged line is descriptive frontmatter, not cryptographic code. I did not find evidence of any weak algorithm or related implementation.

content.value_statement: Complex implementation plans can drift without checkpoints. This skill keeps agents focused on plan review, batch execution, verification, and feedback loops.
actual_capabilities: Guides an agent to read and critique a written implementation plan before starting.
Breaks work into default batches of three tasks.
Requires task status updates, specified verification, and completion reporting.
Prompts the agent to pause for feedback between batches.
Defines blocker conditions that require clarification instead of guessing.
Directs final development completion through a referenced branch finishing skill.
limitations: Requires a written implementation plan before it can be useful.
Does not create the plan or choose architecture by itself.
Depends on the agent and workspace tools for actual implementation and tests.
References another finishing skill that must be available for the final step.

use_cases.title: Implement a prepared engineering plan
use_cases.description: Execute a detailed plan in small batches while preserving review checkpoints and verification steps.

use_cases.title: Coordinate agent work with an architect
use_cases.description: Let an agent complete planned tasks while reporting progress for review between batches.

use_cases.title: Reduce drift during long coding sessions
use_cases.description: Keep implementation aligned with written instructions and pause when blockers or repeated failures appear.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Simple Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use with a complete plan file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill. Read the plan in my workspace, review it for concerns, then execute the first batch with verification.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Plan Before Starting
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill to review this implementation plan. Tell me any blockers or unclear steps before you start work.

prompt_templates.title: Continue After Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Resuming after feedback on a completed batch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill to apply my feedback, then continue with the next batch of planned tasks and report verification results.

prompt_templates.title: Manage a Long Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill for this multi-stage plan. Execute tasks in batches, stop on blockers, and wait for review after each report.

output_examples.input: Execute the first batch from my implementation plan.
output_examples.output: The agent reviews the plan, flags any concerns, completes the first three tasks, runs verification, and reports readiness for feedback.

output_examples.input: A verification step fails twice during the batch.
output_examples.output: The agent stops execution, explains the blocker, shares the failed verification context, and asks for clarification before continuing.

output_examples.input: All planned tasks are completed and verified.
output_examples.output: The agent reports completion and moves to the referenced branch finishing workflow for final checks and user choice.

best_practices: Provide a complete written plan with small, verifiable tasks.
Review each batch report before asking the agent to continue.
Include clear verification commands or acceptance criteria in the plan.
anti_patterns: Using the skill without a written implementation plan.
Asking the agent to skip verification or feedback checkpoints.
Continuing through unclear instructions or repeated test failures.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an agent through a written implementation plan using review, batch execution, verification, and feedback checkpoints.

faq.question: Does it write implementation plans?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to execute an existing plan, not create one from scratch.

faq.question: How large is each work batch?
faq.answer: The default first batch is three tasks, unless the user or plan gives different instructions.

faq.question: When should the agent stop?
faq.answer: The agent should stop for blockers, unclear instructions, repeated verification failures, or critical plan gaps.

faq.question: Does it require external tools?
faq.answer: The skill itself has no code, but the final step references a separate branch finishing skill.

faq.question: Is this useful outside coding work?
faq.answer: It can help with structured task execution, but its language is mainly designed for software implementation plans.
